title: Conflict Stages, Semantic Resolution, and Rerere · Graph Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs conflict stages, semantic resolution, and rerere from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.
Reconstruct intent from base and both histories, produce a semantic combined result, and test invariants that neither side tested alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Index Conflict Stages evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Index Conflict Stages assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G54 is a review in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. Competency probe: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Index Conflict Stages has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Index Conflict Stages precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G54 is a review in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. Competency probe: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Conflict markers contain all base, rename, mode, and stage evidence needed for resolution.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the review Gita Index Conflict Stages case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the review Gita Index Conflict Stages case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the review Gita Index Conflict Stages case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the review Gita Index Conflict Stages case
